info.title: kiapi ERNIE-Image API
info.description:
Image generation, editing, and LoRA training (ERNIE-Image via mflux).

Three operations on Baidu's ERNIE-Image models: `/generate` (text-to-image),
`/edit` (single-image image-to-image), and `/train` (LoRA finetune).

## Upstream docs
- [mflux — ERNIE-Image](https://github.com/filipstrand/mflux/blob/main/src/mflux/models/ernie_image/README.md) — the MLX engine kiapi runs
- [baidu/ERNIE-Image-Turbo](https://huggingface.co/baidu/ERNIE-Image-Turbo) — `turbo` weights
- [baidu/ERNIE-Image](https://huggingface.co/baidu/ERNIE-Image) — `base` weights

## Models
- **turbo** (default) — `baidu/ERNIE-Image-Turbo`. Fast, few steps (default 8),
  guidance 1.0. The everyday choice.
- **base** — `baidu/ERNIE-Image`. Higher quality but slower: more steps
  (default 50) and stronger guidance (default 4.0).

Pick the variant with `model` (aliases like `ernie-image-turbo` are accepted);
discover variants at `GET /v1/image/ernie/models`. Variant-dependent defaults
(`steps` / `guidance` / `quantize`) are filled in server-side when omitted, and
the resolved values are recorded in the result `params`.

## Resident vs transient runs
The warmed, resident model uses the server-default quantization. A request that
either supplies `loras` or sets a `quantize` differing from the resident model
builds a **one-off transient model** for that call — weights load fresh, so it is
slower and not reused. For the fast path, omit `loras` and leave `quantize` unset.

## LoRA workflow
Train an adapter from a captioned image set via `/train` (always async), then
apply it on `/generate` or `/edit` by passing its `adapter_file_id` in `loras`
(up to 4, each `{file, scale}`). Dataset = a ZIP uploaded to the Files API with a
same-stem `.txt` caption next to each image (`cat.png` + `cat.txt`); images may
sit at the top level or in a single subfolder, and `preview*` images are exempt
from the caption requirement.

```
dataset.zip
├── cat_01.png      # image
├── cat_01.txt      # same-stem caption: "a photo of a tabby cat"
├── cat_02.jpg
├── cat_02.txt
└── preview.png     # optional preview, no caption needed
```

A single wrapping subfolder is also accepted (the images just need to live
together under one directory):

```
dataset.zip
└── cats/
    ├── cat_01.png
    ├── cat_01.txt
    └── ...
```

## TIPS
- For a quick image, call `sync` without `Accept: application/json` to get the
  raw bytes straight back (`curl -o out.png`).
- `width`/`height` must be multiples of 16 and at most 2048x2048. `edit`
  additionally requires square sizes by default — set
  `KIAPI_ERNIE_EDIT_REQUIRE_SQUARE=0` to lift the guard (mflux 0.18.0 can fail on
  some non-square ERNIE img2img sizes).
- First use downloads the ERNIE-Image weights; run `kiapi activate` ahead of time
  to avoid a cold-start download on the first request.

## Performance
- **turbo**: q8 512x512 generation around 15s after cache load, peak RSS ~5-6 GiB.
- **base**: slower and uses more steps; prefer turbo unless you need its quality.
- Transient runs (any `loras` or a `quantize` override) rebuild the model each
  call and are slower than the resident path.

post.description:
Generate an image from a text prompt (ERNIE-Image, text-to-image).

No input image — use `/v1/image/ernie/edit` for image-to-image. The same
endpoint serves both `sync` and `async` via `mode`. Variant defaults differ:
`turbo` (default) is fast and uses few steps; `base` is higher quality and
slower.

Sync content negotiation: a single image is produced, so unless the client
asks for JSON the raw image bytes are returned with `X-Kiapi-File-Id` /
`X-Kiapi-Job-Id` headers — `curl -o out.png` just works. With
`Accept: application/json` (or async) the Job JSON is returned, whose
`result` follows ImageResponse.

Any `loras` or a `quantize` differing from the resident model runs a one-off
transient model (slower, not reused). Async returns 202 immediately; poll
GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} and fetch the artifact via GET /v1/files/{file_id}.

parameters.name: Accept

schema.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.
schema.title: Accept

parameters.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.

200.description: Sync result. Returns Job JSON with Accept: application/json; single-artifact jobs may return raw bytes otherwise.

X-Kiapi-File-Id.description: Produced artifact file_id when raw bytes are returned.

X-Kiapi-Job-Id.description: Job id when raw bytes are returned.

400.description: Invalid request for the selected model or file reference.

503.description: Model setup or memory budget error.

504.description: Sync request exceeded the configured timeout.

post.description:
Edit a single input image with a prompt (ERNIE-Image, image-to-image).

Takes one `image` (FileRef) plus a `prompt`; `image_strength` controls how
much of the input is preserved (lower = closer to input). Output must be
square by default (a guard around an mflux 0.18.0 non-square img2img issue),
overrideable via `KIAPI_ERNIE_EDIT_REQUIRE_SQUARE=0`. The same endpoint
serves both `sync` and `async` via `mode`.

Sync content negotiation, transient-model behavior (`loras` / `quantize`
override), and the ImageResponse `result` shape match
`/v1/image/ernie/generate`.

parameters.name: Accept

schema.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.
schema.title: Accept

parameters.description: Response media type preference. application/json returns the Job JSON; otherwise sync requests with one artifact return raw bytes when possible.

200.description: Sync result. Returns Job JSON with Accept: application/json; single-artifact jobs may return raw bytes otherwise.

X-Kiapi-File-Id.description: Produced artifact file_id when raw bytes are returned.

X-Kiapi-Job-Id.description: Job id when raw bytes are returned.

400.description: Invalid request for the selected model or file reference.

503.description: Model setup or memory budget error.

504.description: Sync request exceeded the configured timeout.

post.description:
Train a LoRA adapter on a captioned image set (ERNIE-Image, always async).

Long-running, so this endpoint is always async: it returns 202 with a
job_id — poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}. The `dataset` is a Files-API ZIP whose
images each have a same-stem `.txt` caption. On success the job's `result`
holds `adapter_file_id` (the trained `.safetensors`, also the artifact),
`adapter_bytes`, `num_images`, the resolved `config`, `lora_targets`, and
`timings`; the adapter can then be passed back via `loras` on generate/edit.

503.description: Model setup or memory budget error.

get.description:
List the servable models for this capability.

Returns the public catalog of every variant selectable via the ``model``
field on this capability's endpoints.

schema.title: Response List Models V1 Image Ernie Models Get

job_id.title: Job Id
job_id.description: In-memory job id. Poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id} to inspect status, progress, result, and artifacts.

type.title: Type
type.description: Job type. Generation APIs use values such as zimage, flux2-edit, or acestep-extract.

status.description: Initial job status. Async responses are normally queued unless the worker starts immediately.

name.title: Name
name.description: Model variant name to pass in the request model field.

family.title: Family
family.description: Capability family that resolves this model variant.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Capability domain used for grouping model lists.

aliases.title: Aliases
aliases.description: Alternative names that also resolve to this model.

default.title: Default
default.description: Whether this is the default model when the request omits model.

features.title: Features
features.description: Handler-declared modalities and features supported by this model.

CapabilityModelSpec.title: CapabilityModelSpec
CapabilityModelSpec.description: Public model discovery entry for capability-specific model lists.

model.title: Model
model.description: Model variant (see GET /v1/image/ernie/models). Omit for the default `turbo`; `base` is higher-quality but slower. Aliases such as `ernie-image-turbo` / `ernie-image` are accepted.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: `sync` waits for the image (504 on timeout); `async` returns 202 with a job_id immediately — poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Text prompt describing the desired edit (required).

negative_prompt.title: Negative Prompt
negative_prompt.description: Optional text describing what to avoid in the result.

image.description: Input image to edit, as a Files-API file id, http(s) URL, or data URL.

image_strength.title: Image Strength
image_strength.description: How much the input image is preserved, in 0..1. Lower keeps more of the input; higher follows the prompt more freely.

width.title: Width
width.description: Output width in pixels. Omit for the server default (1024). Must be a multiple of 16 and at most 2048. By default must equal height (square).

height.title: Height
height.description: Output height in pixels. Omit for the server default (1024). Must be a multiple of 16 and at most 2048. By default must equal width (square).

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: Number of denoising steps (1..100). Omit for the variant default (turbo 8, base 50).

guidance.title: Guidance
guidance.description: Classifier-free guidance scale. Omit for the variant default (turbo 1.0, base 4.0); higher follows the prompt more strictly.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. Omit for a random seed (the resolved seed is recorded in the result `params`).

quantize.title: Quantize
quantize.description: Quantization bits, one of {3, 4, 5, 6, 8}. Omit to use the resident model's quantization (default 8). A differing value builds a one-off transient model (slower, not reused).

scheduler.title: Scheduler
scheduler.description: Noise scheduler. `linear` is the default and only tested value.

format.title: Format
format.description: Output image encoding for the produced file.

quality.title: Quality
quality.description: Encoder quality 1..100 (used for jpeg/webp; ignored for png).

loras.title: Loras
loras.description: Up to 4 LoRA adapters [{file, scale}] referencing Files-API ids. Any lora forces a one-off transient model (slower, not reused).

model.title: Model
model.description: Model variant (see GET /v1/image/ernie/models). Omit for the default `turbo`; `base` is the higher-quality, slower variant. Aliases such as `ernie-image-turbo` / `ernie-image` are accepted.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: `sync` waits for the image (504 on timeout); `async` returns 202 with a job_id immediately — poll GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Text prompt describing the image to generate (required).

negative_prompt.title: Negative Prompt
negative_prompt.description: Optional text describing what to avoid in the image.

width.title: Width
width.description: Output width in pixels. Omit for the server default (1024). Must be a multiple of 16 and at most 2048.

height.title: Height
height.description: Output height in pixels. Omit for the server default (1024). Must be a multiple of 16 and at most 2048.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: Number of denoising steps (1..100). Omit for the variant default (turbo 8, base 50). More steps = slower, sometimes higher quality.

guidance.title: Guidance
guidance.description: Classifier-free guidance scale. Omit for the variant default (turbo 1.0, base 4.0); higher follows the prompt more strictly.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. Omit for a random seed (the resolved seed is recorded in the result `params`).

quantize.title: Quantize
quantize.description: Quantization bits, one of {3, 4, 5, 6, 8}. Omit to use the resident model's quantization (default 8). A differing value builds a one-off transient model (slower, not reused).

scheduler.title: Scheduler
scheduler.description: Noise scheduler. `linear` is the default and only tested value.

format.title: Format
format.description: Output image encoding for the produced file.

quality.title: Quality
quality.description: Encoder quality 1..100 (used for jpeg/webp; ignored for png).

loras.title: Loras
loras.description: Up to 4 LoRA adapters [{file, scale}] referencing Files-API ids. Any lora forces a one-off transient model (slower, not reused).

model.title: Model
model.description: Resolved model variant that produced the image (turbo | base).

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: The prompt used for the run.

file_id.title: File Id
file_id.description: Files-API id of the produced image. Fetch metadata at GET /v1/files/{id} or bytes at /download. This is also the artifact returned as raw bytes by a single-artifact sync call.

image_bytes.title: Image Bytes
image_bytes.description: Size of the produced image in bytes.

width.title: Width
width.description: Width in pixels of the produced image.

height.title: Height
height.description: Height in pixels of the produced image.

params.title: Params
params.description: Resolved parameters actually used for the run (model, seed, steps, guidance, quantize, scheduler, format, quality, loras, …), so the result is reproducible.

ImageResponse.title: ImageResponse
ImageResponse.description: Capability-specific ``result`` for a succeeded ernie generate/edit job.

type.description: Job type. Use this to interpret the capability-specific result payload.

params.title: Params
params.description: Request parameters captured for inspection and reproducibility. Secret or large media payloads may be omitted or redacted by endpoints.

id.description: In-memory job id. Jobs are cleared when the kiapi process restarts.

status.description: Job lifecycle state: queued, running, succeeded, failed, or canceled.

artifacts.title: Artifacts
artifacts.description: File ids produced by the job. Use GET /v1/files/{file_id} for metadata or /download for bytes.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message when status is failed; otherwise null.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the job was created.

started_at.title: Started At
started_at.description: Unix timestamp when the worker started the job, or null while queued.

finished_at.title: Finished At
finished_at.description: Unix timestamp when the job reached a terminal state, or null while queued/running.

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: Best-effort completion fraction in [0.0, 1.0]. Null means the job has not reported progress.

progress_label.title: Progress Label
progress_label.description: Short human-readable phase label such as queued, running, denoising, saving, or done.

file.description: The LoRA adapter (`.safetensors`) to apply, as a Files-API file id, http(s) URL, or data URL.

scale.title: Scale
scale.description: Blend strength for this adapter. 1.0 applies it at full weight; lower values weaken its effect, higher values exaggerate it.

LoraRef.title: LoraRef
LoraRef.description:
One LoRA adapter to apply, by Files-API reference and blend scale.

Any lora forces a one-off transient model (slower, not reused). Up to 4 may
be passed in a request's ``loras`` list.

model.title: Model
model.description: Model variant to finetune (see GET /v1/image/ernie/models). Omit for the default `turbo`.

dataset.description: Training dataset as a Files-API reference to a ZIP. Images may sit at the top level or in a single subfolder; each image needs a same-stem `.txt` caption (e.g. `cat.png` + `cat.txt`). `preview*` images are exempt from the caption requirement.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for the training run.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: Inference/denoising steps used during training. Omit for the variant default (turbo 8, base 50).

guidance.title: Guidance
guidance.description: Guidance scale. Omit for the variant default (turbo 1.0, base 4.0).

quantize.title: Quantize
quantize.description: Quantization bits during training, one of {3, 4, 5, 6, 8}. Omit for the variant default (8).

max_resolution.title: Max Resolution
max_resolution.description: Images are resized so their long side is at most this many pixels.

low_ram.title: Low Ram
low_ram.description: Trade speed for lower peak memory during training.

num_epochs.title: Num Epochs
num_epochs.description: Number of passes over the dataset.

timestep_low.title: Timestep Low
timestep_low.description: Lower diffusion-timestep bound to sample. Omit for the default (1).

timestep_high.title: Timestep High
timestep_high.description: Upper diffusion-timestep bound to sample. Omit to use `steps`.

save_frequency.title: Save Frequency
save_frequency.description: Checkpoint every N steps. Omit to only keep the final checkpoint.

lora_rank.title: Lora Rank
lora_rank.description: Rank of the trained LoRA adapter.

lora_include_ff.title: Lora Include Ff
lora_include_ff.description: Also train feed-forward / time-embedding layers (larger adapter), not just attention projections.

lora_layers.title: Lora Layers
lora_layers.description: Advanced: explicit mflux LoRA target spec. Omit to use the built-in default targets derived from `lora_rank` / `lora_include_ff`.

total_s.title: Total S
total_s.description: Wall-clock generation time in seconds (model run only).
